title: On Reading President Lincoln's Letter
content:
To Horace Greeley, of date Aug. 22, 1862 "If I could save the Union without freeing any slave, I would do it," etc.

    Perish the power that, bowed to dust,
    Still wields a tyrant's rod 
    That dares not even then be just,
    And leave the rest with God.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Hanford Lennox Gordon delivers a powerful performance in "On Reading President Lincoln's Letter"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
